title: Credential Compromise: Billing Address Proliferation Across Card Usage
narrative: Stolen card credentials are being monetized through a distributed testing operation where fraudsters cycle through different billing addresses to bypass merchant fraud filters and geographic risk controls. The card's historical signature shows unusually high distinct billing address counts relative to transaction volume, indicating multiple unauthorized actors or address-hopping tactics. Individual transaction amounts remain within the cardholder's normal range to avoid velocity triggers, but the C14 counter (distinct billing addresses) reveals the card has been paired with far more addresses than legitimate usage would produce. Device diversity and elevated C13 (distinct email-domain counts) further expose the fragmented nature of the attack, while per-transaction behavioral signals remain clean. This pattern exploits merchants who rely primarily on AVS matching rather than examining cross-merchant card-aggregation patterns.
indicators: C14 > 7 (p90 threshold) indicating abnormally high distinct billing addresses for the card
C1 > 60 (p90) showing elevated total card-counter aggregation across recent window
C13 > 24 (p90) reflecting high distinct email-domain count associated with the card
card1_distinct_addr1 / C1 ratio > 0.35 indicating disproportionate address diversity per transaction
D1 = 0 or D1 < 3 showing first-time or limited-history device-card pairing
TransactionAmt between 60 and 200 remaining within typical cardholder spending range
C8 > 29 (p90) showing elevated distinct merchant-category interactions for the card
indicator_explanations: The card has been used with far more different billing addresses than normal cardholder behavior
Card-level activity counter shows usage well above the typical baseline for legitimate cardholders
The card has been associated with an unusually high number of different email domains
Billing address changes too frequently relative to total transaction count for legitimate use
The device making this transaction has little to no prior history with this card
Transaction amount fits within the cardholder's normal spending pattern masking the fraud activity
Card has been used across an abnormally diverse set of merchant categories recently
summary: The card shows an abnormally high number of distinct billing addresses relative to transaction volume, combined with elevated email-domain diversity and first-time device usage. Individual transaction amounts remain within normal range, hiding the fraudulent activity from velocity-based controls.
reasoning: Legitimate cardholders rarely change billing addresses frequently across transactions, especially when paired with new devices and diverse email domains. This combination indicates stolen credentials being distributed across multiple fraudsters or tested systematically with address variations to bypass merchant fraud controls.
